data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260506T062901-735b2404/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260506T062901-735b2404/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260506T062901-735b2404/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260506T062901-735b2404/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260506T062901-735b2404/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 2. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-1978065c">
    <title>runs: reuse Story 6 search/chip primitives + Story 7 virtualization</title>
    <description>
Reuse Story 6 search/sort/filter chip primitives (NOT page model). Reuse Story 7 virtualization wrapper for >1k rows; build a Runs-specific adapter on top for live worker progress updates.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Runs page imports chip primitives from artifacts (extracted as shared if needed). 2. Virtualization kicks in past threshold. 3. Live worker phase updates still work through virtualized list.
    </acceptance>
    <notes>
REVIEW:BLOCK

Diff only contains execution manifest/result JSON for the attempt — no actual web/Runs page changes are visible. Cannot evaluate any of the three AC items (chip primitives import, virtualization threshold, live worker phase updates) from this diff.
    </notes>
    <labels>phase:2, story:8, area:web, kind:feature, triage:no-changes-unverified</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-723d93f2" base-rev="dc59efdbfbcd1173105ebf360eb9f8a1a1ceda33" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260506T062901-735b2404"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
No source changes were needed. The current tree already satisfies the Runs reuse/virtualization behavior, so I recorded that as evidence in `.ddx/executions/20260506T062901-735b2404/no_changes_rationale.txt` and committed it as `22c0c239` (`chore(ddx): record no-change evidence [ddx-1978065c]`).

Evidence points:
- [project Runs page](./tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-1978065c-20260506T062901-735b2404/cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/runs/+page.svelte#L10) imports the shared `FilterChip`, sets `VIRTUALIZE_THRESHOLD = 1000`, and wires `subscribeWorkerProgress` plus virtualized row styling at [line 154](./tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-1978065c-20260506T062901-735b2404/cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/runs/+page.svelte#L154).
- [node Runs page](./tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-1978065c-20260506T062901-735b2404/cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/runs/+page.svelte#L10) uses the same shared `FilterChip` and the same virtualization/live-update pattern.
- [artifacts page](./tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-1978065c-20260506T062901-735b2404/cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/+page.svelte#L5) also imports the shared `FilterChip`.
- [runs e2e spec](./tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-1978065c-20260506T062901-735b2404/cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/runs.spec.ts#L596) covers project Runs virtualization and live phase updates, and [line 731](./tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-1978065c-20260506T062901-735b2404/cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/runs.spec.ts#L731) covers the node Runs page using shared chips with the same behavior.

Verification:
- `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun run check` passed.

Repo is clean.
